title: Marlow & Sons Tea audit — strong catalog, leaky discovery and replenishment
context: Sample calibration-grade audit for a fictional Shopify tea store. Structure mirrors the Qosmic target_report bar; used as the renderer and eval golden input.
executive_summary: **Marlow has the catalog; discovery is the leak.** The storefront carries a deep, well-photographed loose-leaf range with 200+ aggregated reviews, but the homepage funnels every first-time visitor through a single ambiguous "Explore" button into a 60-SKU grid. Shoppers are asked to decode tea names instead of shopping by the taste or occasion they actually came for.

**Replenishment is left on the table.** Tea is a monthly consumable, yet product pages offer only one-time purchase and there is no post-order reorder prompt. The brand earns the first order and then goes quiet, leaving repeat revenue — the cheapest revenue it has — uncaptured.

**Performance taxes every page.** The homepage hero ships a 2.4MB uncompressed PNG that gates mobile LCP, and that weight compounds across a catalog browsed mostly on phones. The first test should be structural: convert the homepage into taste-led entry points, then layer in subscriptions and a speed fix.

experiments.title: Add a sticky add-to-cart bar
experiments.affected_surface: Product detail page (pattern applies to all PDPs)
experiments.hypothesis: CVR improves by keeping the add-to-cart action in view as shoppers read tasting notes. The captured PDP pushes the buy button below three scrolls of description, so intent built by the copy has nowhere to land on mobile.
experiments.primary_change: Add a sticky add-to-cart bar on mobile PDPs that appears once the hero image scrolls out of view, showing price, variant, and a single buy button.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if PDP add-to-cart rate improves without hurting PDP bounce rate.

experiments.title: Surface reviews above the fold
experiments.hypothesis: CVR improves by moving the 200+ aggregated reviews next to the price instead of the page footer. Shoppers currently see no social proof until they scroll past the full description, so the strongest trust signal arrives after the decision is made.
experiments.primary_change: Move the review star summary and review count directly under the product title, linking to the full reviews section.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if PDP add-to-cart rate improves without hurting average review submission rate.

experiments.title: Compress the homepage hero
experiments.hypothesis: Bounce drops as largest-contentful-paint improves. The homepage hero ships a 2.4MB uncompressed PNG that delays first render on mobile, and slow first paint is the most common cause of pre-engagement bounce.
experiments.primary_change: Replace the hero PNG with a responsive, compressed WebP and lazy-load below-fold imagery across the homepage.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if mobile LCP improves without hurting homepage click-through.

experiments.title: Bundle a tea + infuser starter kit
experiments.hypothesis: AOV rises by pairing first-time tea buyers with the infuser they need to brew it. The collection sells loose-leaf tins and infusers as separate SKUs with no guided pairing, so new buyers leave with tea and no way to steep it.
experiments.primary_change: Create a "Starter Kit" bundle of a best-selling tin plus a glass infuser at a modest discount, merchandised on collection pages.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if first-time AOV rises by ≥$5 without hurting collection conversion.

experiments.title: Add a free-shipping progress nudge
experiments.hypothesis: AOV rises by nudging carts toward the $45 free-shipping threshold. The cart reveals shipping cost only at checkout, hiding how close a shopper is to free shipping and removing the incentive to add one more tin.
experiments.primary_change: Add a progress bar in the cart drawer showing remaining spend to unlock free shipping, with a one-tap add of a complementary item.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if AOV rises without hurting cart-to-checkout rate.

experiments.title: Build a 'find your tea' quiz
experiments.hypothesis: Landing-page CVR improves by routing flavor-curious visitors to a matched tea before they bounce from a 60-SKU catalog. Collection copy already uses taste descriptors but drops shoppers straight into dense tiles with no guidance.
experiments.primary_change: Create a five-question taste quiz that returns a recommended tea, a tasting note, and an add-to-cart, linked from the primary nav.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if landing-page CVR improves without hurting site-wide conversion.

experiments.title: Launch a brew-guide content hub
experiments.hypothesis: Organic landing CVR improves by capturing "how to brew" search intent on a hub that links to the relevant teas. Steeping guidance is currently buried in scattered blog posts that rank but never route to product.
experiments.primary_change: Create a /brew-guides/ hub aggregating steeping how-tos with an inline product module for each tea type.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if organic landing CVR improves without hurting blog engagement.

experiments.title: Offer a subscribe-and-save option
experiments.hypothesis: Repeat purchase rate improves by letting daily tea drinkers auto-replenish their staple. The PDP offers only one-time purchase despite tea being a consumable bought roughly monthly, so loyal customers must remember to reorder manually.
experiments.primary_change: Add a "Subscribe & save 10%" toggle on consumable PDPs with an editable delivery cadence.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if 90-day repeat purchase rate improves without hurting first-order conversion.

experiments.title: Add a post-purchase reorder reminder
experiments.hypothesis: Reorder rate improves by emailing a one-tap reorder link timed to when a tin typically runs out. There is currently no replenishment prompt after the first order, so repeat demand depends entirely on the customer remembering the brand.
experiments.primary_change: Trigger a reorder-reminder email 25 days post-purchase with a pre-filled cart of the items previously bought.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if 30-day reorder rate improves without hurting email unsubscribe rate.

experiments.title: Clarify the homepage primary CTA
experiments.hypothesis: CVR improves by replacing a vague "Explore" hero button with need-led entry cards. First-time visitors currently face a single ambiguous CTA over a large catalog and have to guess where to begin.
experiments.primary_change: Replace the hero "Explore" button with two cards: "Shop by taste" and "Best sellers," each linking to a curated entry point.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if homepage click-through improves without hurting downstream conversion.

competitor_intro: Competitors win the discovery and replenishment jobs through taste-led navigation, subscriptions, and bundling. Marlow's edge is small-batch sourcing, brew education, and a curated catalog.

competitors.what_they_make_easier: Build-your-own blends and signal sampler packs lower the risk of a first purchase.
competitors.store_edge: Curated small-batch sourcing and stronger brew education.
competitors.pattern_to_adapt: Sampler flights and taste-based discovery quizzes.

competitors.what_they_make_easier: Clear subscription and wholesale paths for recurring buyers.
competitors.store_edge: Deeper origin storytelling and recipe content.
competitors.pattern_to_adapt: Prominent subscribe-and-save on every consumable PDP.

competitors.store_edge: Smaller, more curated catalog that is easier to navigate.

competitors.what_they_make_easier: Editorial brand feel and gifting flows.
competitors.store_edge: More approachable pricing and starter kits.

tech_checks.name: Sitemap
tech_checks.detail: /sitemap.xml resolved with product and collection URLs.

tech_checks.name: Critical Pages Loading
tech_checks.detail: Home, collection, PDP, and cart returned 200.

tech_checks.name: Meta Tags & Social Previews
tech_checks.detail: Title and description present; Open Graph image missing on PDPs.

tech_checks.name: Structured Data
tech_checks.detail: Product schema found; Review/AggregateRating markup absent.

tech_checks.name: Mobile-Friendly
tech_checks.detail: Responsive viewport meta present; layout adapts at 390px.

tech_checks.name: Page Speed Mobile
tech_checks.detail: Lighthouse mobile performance 64; LCP gated by the hero image.

tech_checks.name: Image Optimization
tech_checks.detail: Hero ships a 2.4MB uncompressed PNG with no responsive sources.

tech_checks.name: Cookie/Privacy
tech_checks.detail: Privacy policy linked; consent banner not detected.
